description: Target ES2017 for IIFE builds: eliminates polyfills. -2,052 gzip (-7.5%), -6,701 raw

asi.hypothesis: Target ES2017 for IIFE builds to eliminate __awaiter/__generator polyfills and use native async/await, arrow functions, for-of, let/const
asi.key_insight: Code already requires ES2017+ APIs (async/await, Map, WeakMap, Promise, MutationObserver etc), so ES5 target only adds polyfill bloat

asi.note: Object.freeze on namespace objects is unnecessary in an IIFE where the modules are internal

description: Hoist excludedPrivacyAttributes - no improvement (+2 gzip)

description: Shared coordinates() helper - WORSE +23 gzip despite -264 raw

asi.key_insight: gzip already deduplicates repeated code blocks, so factoring out shared code into helper functions often helps raw size but not gzip size
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on optimizations that reduce unique content rather than deduplicating repeated patterns
asi.rollback_reason: +23 gzip despite -264 raw - gzip handles duplication well

asi.note: clarityOverrides was an internal name attached to window for proxy function storage. Renamed to __clr for compactness.

description: terser property mangle __clr* - UNSAFE: window.t collision risk

asi.hypothesis: terser mangle.properties with regex /^__clr/ to mangle internal __clr* properties
asi.rollback_reason: UNSAFE: window.__clr gets mangled to window.t which could collide with other scripts on the page. Property mangling on window-attached properties is dangerous.
asi.key_insight: Property mangling is only safe for properties that never escape the IIFE scope. Window properties can collide.

description: terser passes=4 - no change from passes=3

asi.rollback_reason: No change

description: Replace indexOf boolean checks with includes() across 27 occurrences. -52 gzip, -46 raw

asi.hypothesis: Replace indexOf(x)>=0/indexOf(x)<0 with includes(x)/!includes(x) across ~27 occurrences in ~15 files. In ES2020, includes() compiles natively and produces shorter minified output than indexOf-comparison patterns.

description: Store Granted/Denied in const vars - FAILS: TDZ due to circular deps

asi.hypothesis: Store frequently used const enum strings (Granted/Denied) in module-level const variables to reduce inlining repetition
asi.key_insight: FAILS due to TDZ: circular module dependencies cause const variables to be accessed before initialization. const enum inlining avoids this because values are compile-time constants not runtime variables.
asi.rollback_reason: Tests fail: ReferenceError: Cannot access 'nr' before initialization — circular module deps cause TDZ

description: terser reduce_vars - no change

asi.hypothesis: terser reduce_vars
asi.rollback_reason: No change

description: ||= operator in scrub.ts - +3 gzip worse

asi.rollback_reason: +3 gzip - ||= adds unique byte pattern that gzip can't compress as well

description: hash.ts: Math.pow(2,n) → 1<<n bitwise shift. -1 gzip, -7 raw

asi.hypothesis: Replace Math.pow(2, precision) with (1 << precision) bitwise shift in hash.ts
asi.note: Bitwise shift is a direct CPU operation and produces shorter minified output than Math.pow

description: Math.pow→literal in shortid - +2 gzip, literal compresses worse

asi.hypothesis: Replace Math.pow(2,32) with literal 4294967296 in metadata.ts shortid()
asi.rollback_reason: +2 gzip - gzip compresses Math.pow pattern better than literal number
asi.key_insight: Large numeric literals (10 digits) compress worse under gzip than common function calls because they introduce unique byte sequences

description: compress.ts: use Response API to consume stream, eliminate read(). -44 gzip, -113 raw

asi.hypothesis: Replace manual stream reader loop in compress.ts with Response API: new Response(stream).arrayBuffer(). Eliminates the entire read() function.
asi.key_insight: Response API can consume a ReadableStream in one line, replacing a 10-line async loop function. Both have same browser support requirements.

description: document.ts dim() helper - +13 gzip despite -82 raw

asi.hypothesis: Extract dim() helper in document.ts to deduplicate width/height measurement
asi.key_insight: AGAIN: gzip compresses repeated inline patterns well. Helper functions add unique call overhead that increases gzip despite reducing raw size.
asi.rollback_reason: +13 gzip despite -82 raw

asi.key_insight: MIXED: raw -183 but gzip +10. The entry.data. pattern is repeated many times and gzip deduplicates it well. Adding local aliases breaks the pattern.

description: Replace import * as → named imports for modules[] in clarity.ts. -28 gzip but checks timed out

asi.hypothesis: Replace import * as X with named imports {start, stop} for diagnostic, interaction, layout, performance, dynamic in clarity.ts. This eliminates 5 namespace objects that contain ALL exports.
asi.note: PROMISING: -28 gzip, -220 raw. But checks timed out on first run. Tests pass individually. Need rerun.

description: clarity.ts: named imports for modules[] eliminating 5 namespace objects. -28 gzip, -220 raw

asi.hypothesis: Replace import * as X with named imports {start, stop} for 5 module namespaces in clarity.ts. Eliminates rollup namespace objects for diagnostic, interaction, layout, performance, dynamic.
asi.key_insight: import * as creates namespace objects with ALL module exports. Using named imports with explicit {start, stop} object literals gives rollup the chance to tree-shake the namespace.

description: data/index.ts: named imports for 13 sub-modules eliminating namespace bloat. -324 gzip, -1865 raw

asi.hypothesis: Replace 13 import * as with named imports in data/index.ts — eliminates 13 namespace objects that contained ALL module exports. The modules[] array only needs {start, stop}. compute() calls converted to direct named function calls.
asi.key_insight: This was the BIGGEST remaining win. 13 data sub-module namespaces (baseline, dimension, variable, limit, summary, cookie, consent, metadata, envelope, upload, ping, upgrade, extract) were all converted from full namespace objects to minimal {start,stop} pairs.

description: Named imports in layout/interaction/diagnostic/performance index.ts — no additional gzip change

asi.hypothesis: Convert import * as to named imports in layout/index.ts (8 modules), interaction/index.ts (14 modules), diagnostic/index.ts (3 modules), performance/index.ts (2 modules)
asi.note: Same as previous run (24,170) — the data/index.ts change from run 62 was the big win. These additional index files had no further namespace overhead because rollup had already inlined them after the clarity.ts change eliminated the top-level namespace reference.

description: Session 6 start: confirmed 24,170 gzip / 66,529 raw (-12.7%)

asi.hypothesis: Session 6 baseline confirmation

description: node.ts: eliminate dom namespace via named imports + domCall helper. -92 gzip, -558 raw

asi.hypothesis: Eliminate dom namespace in node.ts by converting import * as dom to named imports + domCall() helper replacing dom[call] dynamic dispatch. This eliminates the last unnecessary namespace object.
asi.key_insight: The dom[call] dynamic dispatch pattern forced Rollup to keep the entire dom namespace as a runtime object. Replacing with a boolean flag + explicit function calls allows Rollup to inline everything.

description: core/index.ts named imports - no change, Rollup already inlined

asi.hypothesis: Convert 7 import * as to named imports in core/index.ts
asi.rollback_reason: No change - Rollup already inlined these since namespaces weren't used as values
asi.note: Only namespace objects used as VALUES (passed to functions, stored in arrays) create overhead. Method-only usage is already inlined.
